title: Cattle & Customer Management System — Frontend Rebuild with Admin and Customer Portals

requirements.summary: Build Admin Panel frontend with protected login, Cattle Management table with CRUD operations, Customer Management table with active/inactive toggle, and Orders & Deliveries table with status updates
acceptanceCriteria: Admin login screen is shown to unauthenticated users navigating to /admin
Correct admin credentials grant access to the admin panel
Cattle table displays all fields and supports add/edit/delete via dialogs
Customer table supports add/edit/delete and active/inactive toggle
Orders table displays all orders and allows status updates
Admin is redirected to login on logout or session expiry

file_operations.description: Create the main admin panel page component that uses the authorization component to enforce admin-only access. Use Internet Identity login as the authentication mechanism (admins are principals with the admin role assigned via the authorization component). After successful authentication and role verification, render a tabbed interface with three sections: Cattle Management, Customer Management, and Orders & Deliveries. Use shadcn/ui Tabs component for navigation between sections. Show a login prompt with Internet Identity integration for unauthenticated users. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a cattle management section component for the admin panel that calls getAllCattle() from the backend interface and displays a data table with columns: tagNumber, breed, dateOfPurchase (formatted timestamp), milkingCapacity (litres/day), purchasePrice, availability (Available/Sold/Reserved badge), healthStatus (Healthy/Sick/Recovered badge), and action buttons. Include 'Add Cattle' button that opens a dialog with a form for all seven cattle fields. Each row has Edit and Delete action buttons that call updateCattle() and deleteCattle() backend methods respectively. Use shadcn/ui Table, Dialog, Button, Badge, and Form components. Show loading and error states. Use TanStack Query hooks for data fetching and mutations.

Create a customer management section component that calls getAllCustomers() from the backend interface and displays a table with columns: name, phone, address, isActive (toggle switch), and action buttons. Include 'Add Customer' button opening a dialog with fields for name, phone, address, username, password (with hash on submit), and isActive checkbox. Each row has Edit and Delete buttons calling updateCustomer() and deleteCustomer(). The isActive toggle calls setCustomerActive(customerId, newStatus). Use shadcn/ui Table, Dialog, Switch, Button, and Form components. Hash passwords client-side before sending to backend. Show appropriate loading and error states.

Create orders and deliveries section component that calls getAllOrders() from the backend interface and displays a table with columns: orderId, customer name (resolve from customerId by cross-referencing getAllCustomers()), cattleTagNumber, orderDate (formatted timestamp), status (Pending/Confirmed/OutForDelivery/Delivered/Cancelled badge), and deliveryNotes. Include an 'Update Status' button per row that opens a dialog allowing status change via dropdown and optional deliveryNotes update, calling updateOrderStatus(orderId, newStatus, notes). Use shadcn/ui Table, Dialog, Select, Textarea, Badge, and Button components. Show loading and error states.

Create TanStack Query hooks for admin operations: useGetAllCattle (calls actor.getAllCattle), useAddCattle (mutation calling actor.addCattle), useUpdateCattle (mutation calling actor.updateCattle), useDeleteCattle (mutation calling actor.deleteCattle), useGetAllCustomers (calls actor.getAllCustomers), useAddCustomer (mutation calling actor.addCustomer with client-side password hashing), useUpdateCustomer (mutation calling actor.updateCustomer), useDeleteCustomer (mutation calling actor.deleteCustomer), useSetCustomerActive (mutation calling actor.setCustomerActive), useGetAllOrders (calls actor.getAllOrders), useUpdateOrderStatus (mutation calling actor.updateOrderStatus). Include proper query invalidation on mutations and toast notifications on success/error.

Add a new route for /admin that renders the AdminPanel page component. Ensure the route is registered in the TanStack Router configuration alongside existing routes. Do not remove or modify existing routes (dashboard, cattle, milk, inventory, customers, deliveries, reports, /portal).
Add a navigation link to 'Admin Panel' (/admin) in both desktop and mobile navigation menus. Position it logically within the existing navigation structure. Do not remove existing links.

requirements.summary: Build Customer Portal frontend at /portal with username/password login, Place Order tab for selecting available cattle and submitting orders, and My Deliveries tab listing customer's own orders with status badges
acceptanceCriteria: Customer login form is shown at /portal for unauthenticated users
Active, admin-created customers can log in with correct credentials
Inactive customers see an 'Access denied / account inactive' message after login attempt
Place Order tab shows available cattle and submits an order
My Deliveries tab shows only the logged-in customer's orders with current statuses
Customers cannot access cattle inventory details beyond tag number and breed for ordering

file_operations.description: Replace the existing Internet Identity authentication flow with a custom username/password login form. When unauthenticated, display a login form with username and password fields that calls customerLogin(username, password) from the backend interface. On successful login (receiving a session token), store the token and customerId in React state or context. Handle LoginError variants explicitly: show 'Account not found' for AccountNotFound, 'Invalid credentials' for InvalidCredentials, and 'Access denied - your account is inactive' for AccountInactive. After successful login, render the existing tabbed interface (product catalog renamed to 'Place Order', delivery history renamed to 'My Deliveries'). Remove Internet Identity provider dependency from this page. Keep the ProfileSetupModal removed or hidden as it's not needed for username/password customers.

Create a Place Order tab component that fetches available cattle via getAllCattle() and filters for availability === 'Available'. Display cattle in a card grid or selectable list showing only tagNumber and breed (no other inventory details). Provide a form with a cattle selection dropdown, delivery notes textarea, and a 'Place Order' button. On submit, call placeOrder(customerId, selectedCattleTagNumber, deliveryNotes) where customerId comes from the logged-in session state. Show success toast on order placement and refresh the My Deliveries tab data. Use shadcn/ui Card, Select, Textarea, and Button components.

Create a My Deliveries tab component that calls getMyOrders() from the backend interface (returns only the logged-in customer's orders). Display orders in a table or list with columns: orderId, cattleTagNumber, orderDate (formatted), status (badge with color coding: Pending=yellow, Confirmed=blue, OutForDelivery=purple, Delivered=green, Cancelled=red), and deliveryNotes. Use shadcn/ui Table and Badge components. Show loading and empty states appropriately.

Create TanStack Query hooks for customer operations: useCustomerLogin (mutation calling actor.customerLogin, returning token and handling LoginError variants), useGetAvailableCattle (query calling actor.getAllCattle and filtering for Available status), usePlaceOrder (mutation calling actor.placeOrder with customerId, cattleTagNumber, deliveryNotes), useGetMyOrders (query calling actor.getMyOrders). Include proper error handling and toast notifications.

Create a React context and provider to manage customer authentication state (isAuthenticated, customerId, sessionToken, username). Provide login and logout functions that wrap the useCustomerLogin mutation and manage session state. Store session data in memory (React state) and optionally sessionStorage for persistence across page reloads. Provide a useCustomerAuth hook for consuming components.

Wrap the App component with the new CustomerAuthProvider to make customer authentication state available throughout the customer portal pages. Place it inside the existing QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider wrappers.

requirements.summary: Apply a consistent green-and-white visual theme with earthy accents across Admin Panel and Customer Portal, ensuring the admin panel feels authoritative and data-dense while the customer portal feels friendly and simple
acceptanceCriteria: Color palette is applied consistently across all pages
Admin panel and customer portal share a coherent but role-appropriate visual style
All tables, forms, and dialogs use the defined theme

file_operations.description: Update the existing CSS custom properties to define a green-and-white color palette with earthy accent tones. Replace or extend the current oklch-based color system to include primary green shades (e.g., --primary: 142 70% 45% for a professional green), white backgrounds, and earthy accents (browns, tans for secondary elements). Ensure both light and dark mode tokens are updated. Keep the existing Tailwind directives and font imports intact. The updated palette should support authoritative data-dense admin interfaces and friendly simple customer interfaces.

Extend the Tailwind theme configuration to reference the updated CSS custom properties from index.css for the green-and-white palette. Add or update color aliases (e.g., 'admin-primary', 'customer-primary', 'earthy-accent') that map to the new green and earthy tones. Ensure existing chart, farm, and sidebar color palettes are preserved or adapted to the new theme. Do not remove existing plugins or font configurations.

Apply admin-specific styling using the defined green-and-white theme. Use darker, more authoritative shades for headers and navigation. Apply card-based layouts with generous padding and clear section dividers to convey a data-dense, professional feel. Use shadcn/ui components styled with the updated Tailwind classes referencing the new color palette.

Apply customer-specific styling using softer, friendlier tones from the green-and-white palette. Use lighter green accents and larger, more approachable typography. Simplify the layout with clear calls-to-action and ample whitespace to create a welcoming, easy-to-navigate experience. Use shadcn/ui components styled with the updated Tailwind classes.
